title: Tom Jung

|death=

'''Tom Jung''' (born [[February 13]], [[1940s#1942|1942]]) is an American art director, graphic designer, and illustrator. He is noted as the designer of the [[:File:Star Wars Style A poster 1977.jpg|''Style A'']] poster advertising the theatrical release of ''Star Wars'' in US cinemas.<ref name="postercollector" />

Jung is credited with designing a number of film posters, including ''Plan 9 from Outer Space'' (1959), ''Doctor Zhivago ''(1965),'' Gone With The Wind ''(1967 re-release), ''The Omen ''(1976) and'' The Lord of the Rings ''(1978).<ref name="postercollector" />

==Biography==
[[File:Star_Wars_Style_A_poster_1977.jpg|thumb|left|Tom Jung's 1977 ''Style A'' poster for ''Star Wars'']]
In 1976, [[20th Century Studios|20th Century Fox]] commissioned Smolen, Smith and Connolly to produce posters to promote the forthcoming film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'']], and Jung was chosen to produce the artwork. He was given the brief of "good over evil," and provided with a wealth of photos taken by unit photographers in color and black & white, as well as 2.25-inch stills on contact sheets taken from the original 35mm print of the film. Jung's work was used as the one-sheet [[:File:Star Wars Style B poster 1977.jpg|''Style A'' theatrical poster]] for the film's advertising campaign. According to Jung, the unlikely "cross" formed by Luke Skywalker's saber sword set against the ghosted background image of Darth Vader seemed to him like a good solution to the "good" versus "evil" theme.<ref name="filmschoolrejects">{{WebCite|url=https://filmschoolrejects.com/star-wars-the-posters-14ad09654325|text=The History of Star Wars Posters|author=Dass, William|date=2016-12-14|work=Film School Rejects|archivedate=20200807144518}}</ref><ref name="scifimovieposters">{{WebCite|url=http://www.sci-fimovieposters.co.uk/british-star-wars-posters.htm|text=A short history of the first British Star Wars posters|work=www.sci-fimovieposters.co.uk|archivedate=20200807211137}}</ref><ref name="postercollector" />

A reworked version of Jung's design, [[:File:Star Wars Style B poster 1977.jpg|the ''Style B'' poster]], was produced by the [[Brothers Hildebrandt]] for the UK release of ''Star Wars'', followed by the [[:File:Star Wars style C poster 1977.jpg|''Style C'' poster]] designed by [[Tom Chantrell]].<ref name="filmschoolrejects" />

In 1980, Lucasfilm commissioned him to design a theater poster for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], depicting a standing [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]] with a billowing cape. Jung also created an unused poster concept for [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']], which featured an image of the [[DS-2 Death Star II Mobile Battle Station|second Death Star]] exploding.<ref name="filmschoolrejects" /><ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.sciencefictionarchives.com/en/collections/313/return-of-the-jedi-1985-rerelease-one-sheet-poster-original-painting-by-tom-jung|text=Return of the Jedi 1985 Rerelease One-Sheet Poster Original Painting by Tom Jung|work=Science fiction Archives|nobackup=1}}</ref>

Jung drew the covers for several books: ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', ''[[Dark Force Rising]]'', and ''[[The Last Command]]''. He also drew the back cover for the ''Star Wars'' original soundtrack.
